frames: The agent stands upright, then begins to tilt slightly to its right. Its base remains flat on the checkered ground.
The agent continues to lean further to its right, maintaining full contact with the ground via its base. The motion is smooth.
The upper segment starts to bend at the joint as the entire structure leans more significantly to the right. The base is still firmly on the ground.
The agent's upper body bends further, and the entire structure is now leaning at a steep angle. The base maintains ground contact.
The agent's upper segment is almost horizontal, and the structure is heavily tilted to the right. The base remains on the ground.
The upper segment is now fully horizontal, and the entire agent is pivoting on its base. The motion appears to be a controlled fall.
The agent's upper segment remains horizontal, and the base is still in contact with the ground, sliding slightly. The agent is not performing a locomotion task.
The agent continues to lie horizontally, with its base still on the ground, indicating a complete loss of upright posture.
